assumptions: Task description was provided and only used as a low-risk planning hint.
Spec text was provided and only used where it did not contradict the contract.

expected_properties: Observed outputs or protocol-visible signals settle to a stable, non-X post-reset state.
No fixed-cycle completion is assumed during reset recovery.
Hours, Mins, Secs are forced to 0 when RST is asserted high
Outputs remain stable (no X) throughout reset assertion and release
cases.goal: Confirm that the active‑high reset reliably initializes all time counters to zero.
notes: Reset polarity may still be heuristic if the contract marks it ambiguous.
Reset polarity is taken from contract (active high).
Observes several clock cycles while reset is held to ensure no spurious transitions.
preconditions: Reset signal is available in the contract.
stimulus_intent: Assert the detected reset using the inferred polarity.
Release reset conservatively and observe interface stabilization.
Assert RST for a few clock cycles, then deassert and verify outputs settle to 0.
timing_assumptions: Do not assume completion before it becomes externally visible.
Allow at least two clock edges while reset is asserted to cover any synchronous reset latency.

expected_properties: Externally visible outputs remain defined and can change across conservative clocked observation windows.
The plan observes progress without assuming a fixed-cycle protocol or hidden internal state access.
Hours, Mins, Secs remain defined (non-X) after reset release
Secs increments by 1 each clock cycle until 59 then wraps to 0
Mins increments by 1 when Secs wraps from 59 to 0
Hours increments by 1 when Mins and Secs both wrap from 59 to 0
cases.goal: Verify normal time‑keeping progression under nominal clocking after a clean reset.
notes: Case intent is conservative when the contract is weak or timing is unresolved.
Case relies on deterministic clock-driven observation because no non-control inputs were resolved.
Observes a small window of clock cycles (e.g., 70 cycles) to capture a full minute rollover.
No assumptions about exact latency; only that changes become externally visible on clock edges.
preconditions: Use only ports and observable signals present in the contract.
stimulus_intent: Release reset if present and observe a small number of conservative clock edges for externally visible progress.
Release reset and let the clock run for enough cycles to see seconds, minute, and hour rollovers.
timing_assumptions: Do not assume completion before it becomes externally visible.
Conservative observation across successive positive edges of CLK.
No fixed‑cycle latency assumed for rollovers.

expected_properties: Secs wraps from 59 to 0 on the next clock edge
Mins increments from 59 to 0 when Secs wraps at 59
Hours increments from 23 to 0 when Mins and Secs both wrap at 59
cases.goal: Validate correct wrap‑around behavior at the second, minute, and hour boundaries.
notes: Stimulus drives the clock long enough to reach 23:59:58, then observes the next two cycles.
No assumptions about exact number of cycles to reach the boundary; the test monitors values until the condition is met.
Case relies on deterministic clock-driven observation because no non-control inputs were resolved.
preconditions: Reset has been released and the design is in a stable post‑reset state.
stimulus_intent: Run the clock until the time reaches 23:59:58, then continue to capture the transition to 23:59:59 and subsequently to 00:00:00.
timing_assumptions: Observe over a sufficient number of clock edges; no fixed‑cycle latency is assumed for the rollovers.

expected_properties: When RST is asserted for a single clock cycle and then deasserted, Hours, Mins, Secs return to 0 and remain stable
No metastability or spurious increments occur during rapid reset deassertion
cases.goal: Check robustness of the design against back‑to‑back reset assertions.
notes: The test asserts RST for one clock edge, deasserts it, and then observes the outputs for several cycles.
Case relies on deterministic clock-driven observation because no non-control inputs were resolved.
preconditions: Design is powered and clock is running.
stimulus_intent: Pulse RST high for exactly one CLK rising edge, then release and monitor outputs.
timing_assumptions: Assume synchronous reset; observe at least three clock cycles after deassertion.

unresolved_items: Spec/reset hint could not be mapped to a known reset port: The calendar module uses three always blocks to update the values of seconds, minutes, and hours based on the clock signal and reset signal.

Spec/reset hint could not be mapped to a known reset port: The second always block also triggers on the positive edge of the clock signal or the positive edge of the reset signal. It handles the minutes value (Mins). If the reset signal is active, it sets the minutes value to 0. If both the minutes and seconds values are 59, it wraps around and sets the minutes value to 0. If the seconds value is 59, it increments the minutes value by 1. Otherwise, it keeps the minutes value unchanged.

Spec/reset hint could not be mapped to a known reset port: The third always block triggers on the positive edge of the clock signal or the positive edge of the reset signal. It handles the hours value (Hours). If the reset signal is active, it sets the hours value to 0. If the hours, minutes, and seconds values are all at their maximum (23, 59, and 59 respectively), it wraps around and sets the hours value to 0. If the minutes and seconds values are both 59, it increments the hours value by 1. Otherwise, it keeps the hours value unchanged.
Contract strength is limited; generated cases favor safe observation over precise timing assumptions.
